description: Lecture discussing the CAP theorem, its properties, and implications for distributed systems. The CAP theorem states that in the presence of a network partition, a system can only guarantee two of the three properties: consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.
topic_summary: The CAP theorem, articulated by Eric Brewer and later proved by Seth Gilbert and Nancy Lynch, outlines the trade-offs in distributed systems. It emphasizes that during a network partition, a system must choose between consistency and availability [t=211.99].
